paper_title: Analysis of the A. formosa genome and comparison with CCM components in other diatoms

methods.description: Process for cultivating A. formosa and preparing samples for genomic and RNA extraction.

steps.description: A culture of A. formosa from Esthwaite Water (CCAP 1005/24) was grown at 16.5°C with a 12:12 h light:dark cycle illuminated with a photon irradiance of 50 µmol photon m −2 s −1 and shaken at 110 rpm.
steps.searchable_summary: grow A. formosa culture under specified conditions

steps.description: Genomic DNA and RNA were extracted from cultures in exponential phase containing about 2.5 × 10^7 and 10^8 cells ml −1 of A. formosa, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: extract genomic DNA and RNA from A. formosa cultures

steps.description: Cultures were filtered on 8-µm and then 0.22-µm membranes, frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at -80°C.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and store A. formosa cultures
outputs: filtered and stored A. formosa samples

methods.description: Protocols for extracting DNA and RNA from A. formosa cultures.

steps.description: DNA was extracted following a hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB)-based protocol adapted from (Bruckner et al. 2008).
RNA was extracted using TRI-reagent (Sigma-Aldrich, St. Louis, Missouri, USA) and the SV total RNA isolation system (Promega, Madison, Wisconsin, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: extract RNA using TRI-reagent and SV total RNA isolation system

methods.description: Sequencing and assembly of the A. formosa genome using Illumina and Pacific Biosciences technologies.

steps.description: DNA sequencing was performed using Illumina NextSeq 500 technology.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DNA sequencing with Illumina NextSeq 500

steps.description: Sequencing was completed by RNAseq to annotate the eukaryotic genes that contain exons and introns.
Fourteen single-molecule real-time cells on a Pacific Biosciences RSII sequencer were used to improve sequencing quality of the genome.
steps.searchable_summary: use Pacific Biosciences RSII for high-quality genome sequencing

steps.description: Genome assembly was performed using the hierarchical genome-assembly process software HGAP.

methods.description: Comparison of A. formosa genome with existing databases to identify genes encoding components of the CCM.

steps.description: The genome of A. formosa was compared to existing databases to identify genes encoding components of the CCM: all subclasses of carbonic anhydrase (CA) and SLC families 4 and 26 (SLC4 and SLC26).
steps.searchable_summary: compare A. formosa genome with databases for CCM components

steps.description: Performed a BLAST search using BioEdit software and proteins of Phaeodactylum tricornutum Bohlin as queries.
steps.searchable_summary: perform BLAST search with BioEdit using Phaeodactylum tricornutum proteins

steps.description: The identity of each protein was checked and aligned using Multiple Sequence Comparison by Log-Expectation (MUSCLE).
Alignments were imported into Genedoc and similar residues were shaded using the conservation mode.
steps.searchable_summary: import alignments into Genedoc and shade similar residues

methods.description: Prediction of the topology and localization of SLC and CA proteins in A. formosa.

steps.description: The topology of the putative SLCs was predicted using TMHMM v2 to confirm that they were transmembrane proteins.
steps.searchable_summary: predict SLC protein topology using TMHMM v2

steps.description: The 2D structure was predicted using Hydrophobic Cluster Analysis (HCA).
The location of the CA and SLC proteins was determined, based on their signal peptides, using HECTAR.
